source: Oztas et al. 2023 — Synthetic AML Transaction Monitoring Dataset (SAML-D)
license: CC BY 4.0 (per Kaggle dataset license field)

SAML-D.csv.notes: 12 features per row. 28 typologies in `Laundering_type` (11 normal, 17 suspicious). `Is_laundering` is the binary label.

pipeline.elixir_consumer: mix corpus.generate.saml_d (reads saml_d.ndjson; produces sharded AH/CP/PA/Txn ndjson under --out)

notes: No direct download — Kaggle CLI auth (kaggle.json) required. Subset to NDJSON happens in the Makefile via pandas so Elixir never reads the 12 MB raw CSV; only the small ndjson sample enters the mix task. Per-row checksum is not pinned because the upstream is mutable on Kaggle; pin a `fetched_ref` instead.
